name: Buri
description: Buri is a primordial being in Norse mythology, considered the first of the Æsir gods. He emerged from the salty ice blocks of Ginnungagap, the primordial void that existed before the creation of the world. Licked free from the ice by the primeval cow Auðumbla, Buri represents the very genesis of the gods' lineage. He is the father of Borr, who in turn fathered Odin, Vili, and Vé – the principal gods responsible for shaping the cosmos. Buri's existence signifies the transition from chaos to order, from the formless void to the structured world of Norse cosmology. While details about his personality and specific powers are scarce, his importance lies in his role as the progenitor of the gods. He embodies the raw potential and latent power that existed before the world was formed. His emergence from the ice symbolizes the birth of life and consciousness from the frozen depths of the primordial void. The act of Auðumbla licking him free suggests a nurturing force that brings forth this first being. Although Buri himself does not feature prominently in Norse myths beyond his origin, his existence is foundational to the entire Norse pantheon and their subsequent actions. He is a silent but crucial figure, representing the very beginning of the divine lineage that would shape the fate of the cosmos. His legacy is carried on through his descendants, the Æsir gods, who would eventually battle giants, create humanity, and ultimately face Ragnarök.
subtitle: The First of the Gods

keyMyths.title: The Emergence of Buri
keyMyths.description: Buri emerged from the salty ice blocks of Ginnungagap, licked free by the primeval cow Auðumbla. He is considered the first of the Æsir gods, marking the beginning of their lineage.

keyMyths.title: Father of Borr
keyMyths.description: Buri fathered Borr, who then married the giantess Bestla and fathered Odin, Vili, and Vé. This established the lineage of the principal Æsir gods.

keyMyths.title: Buri's Legacy
keyMyths.description: Although details are scarce, Buri's existence is fundamental. His descendants, Odin, Vili and Vé, shaped the cosmos and fought against chaos, ensuring his legacy.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source of Norse mythology, including the creation myth and Buri's origin.
